title: Tumour recurrence, gastrointestinal symptoms and inflammation associated with microplastic-positive intestinal tumours
data_sources: Clinical tumour specimens (188 intestinal tumours, China)
WHO Microplastics in Drinking Water report (2019)
core_conclusion: Microplastics were detected in 56.4% of 188 clinical intestinal tumour specimens using complementary high-resolution spectroscopic and imaging techniques, with PET as the dominant polymer. MP-positive tumours displayed localized inflammation and histopathological changes, and patients with MP-positive tumours showed a trend toward higher recurrence rates and significantly elevated gastrointestinal symptom severity over 34 months of follow-up, implicating MPs as a potential contributor to colorectal cancer burden.
why_it_matters: Provides first rigorous in situ multi-modal spectroscopic evidence of MPs in human intestinal tumour tissue; links MP positivity to clinical outcomes (recurrence trend and symptom severity); quantifies the MP-inflammation axis in human cancer tissue; elevates the evidence base for environmental regulation of plastic-contact materials.
why_nature: Addresses a globally salient public-health concern with translational human tissue evidence; combines three orthogonal analytical techniques setting a new methodological standard; prospective 34-month follow-up elevates study design above detection-only studies; cross-disciplinary synthesis of environmental science, gastroenterology, and oncology.
added_value_to_my_research: Spatial exposomics: geocode patient addresses and link MP tissue burden to residential environmental exposure layers via MGWR/GWR spatial regression
Explainable ML (SHAP) classifier to predict MP positivity from clinical, dietary, and spatial features at patient intake
Gut microbiome as mediator: physics-informed graph learning on microbiome networks to model the MP-inflammation-recurrence causal pathway
Health-economic burden modeling: spatially disaggregate the population-level cost of MP-driven cancer recurrence for policy cost-benefit analysis
Mobile-phone GPS trajectories + spatiotemporal KDE to reconstruct cumulative environmental MP exposure histories for individual patients

figures.caption: Overview of total MP abundances in all tissue samples from intestinal tumours — distribution of MP counts and polymer types across 188 specimens
MPs detected in situ within intestinal tissue sections using RISE and HSI — spatially resolved spectroscopic images showing MP particle locations within tumour tissue
Elevated inflammatory biomarkers observed in tumour tissues associated with MPs — co-localisation of inflammatory markers with MP particles in histopathological sections
Associations between the presence of MPs and intestinal health — clinical outcome comparisons (tumour recurrence trend and GSRS scores) between MP-positive and MP-negative patient groups
